title: Alternate Audit Capability During Control Outages

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 AU-15 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AU-15 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.14: Alternate Audit Capability During Control Outages.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.14' running on _internal pipeline errors, index=net tap sourcetypes, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Connect for Syslog
spl:
index=_internal OR index=net earliest=-24h
| eval channel=case(match(sourcetype,"stream"),"PCAP_TAP", match(sourcetype,"syslog"),"SYSLOG_ALT",1=1,"PRIMARY")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(eval(if(channel="PRIMARY",1,0))) as primary_hits,
        sum(eval(if(channel!="PRIMARY",1,0))) as alt_hits by _time, host
| eval alt_ratio=round(alt_hits/(primary_hits+alt_hits+0.001),3)
| where alt_ratio>0.3
description: Detects when primary logging pipelines fail and compensating syslog or packet capture taps increase, evidencing AU-15 alternate audit capability activation.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes _internal, net. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Label alternate paths consistently; (2) tune ratio for your architecture; (3) require ticket linkage when alt_ratio spikes; (4) review monthly with contingency tests (CP-10). Indexes required: `index=_internal`, `index=net`.
visualization: Time chart (alt_ratio), Table (hosts), Single value (minutes in compensating mode)
knownFalsePositives: Audit log gaps during planned forwarder maintenance, scheduled retention rotations, or platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Connect for Syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` pipeline errors, `index=net` tap sourcetypes.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Label alternate paths consistently; (2) tune ratio for your architecture; (3) require ticket linkage when alt_ratio spikes; (4) review monthly with contingency tests (CP-10).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal OR index=net earliest=-24h
| eval channel=case(match(sourcetype,"stream"),"PCAP_TAP", match(sourcetype,"syslog"),"SYSLOG_ALT",1=1,"PRIMARY")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(eval(if(channel="PRIMARY",1,0))) as primary_hits,
        sum(eval(if(channel!="PRIMARY",1,0))) as alt_hits by _time, host
| eval alt_ratio=round(alt_hits/(primary_hits+alt_hits+0.001),3)
| where alt_ratio>0.3
```

Understanding this SPL

**Alternate Audit Capability During Control Outages (AU-15)** — Detects when primary logging pipelines fail and compensating syslog or packet capture taps increase, evidencing AU-15 alternate audit capability activation.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` pipeline errors, `index=net` tap sourcetypes. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Connect for Syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal, net.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_internal, index=net, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **channel** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **alt_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where alt_ratio>0.3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT historian (PI, Proficy) or the vendor HMI/operations console the operator trusts. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (alt_ratio), Table (hosts), Single value (minutes in compensating mode)
grandmaExplanation: We detects when primary logging pipelines fail and compensating syslog or packet capture taps increase, evidencing AU-15 alternate audit capability activation. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
